description_long: This object is a vintage tabletop radio receiver with a rectangular wooden body and a polished, dark wood grain finish. The front panel features a speaker grille on the left, three round control knobs along the bottom, and a frequency display window with tuning markings and a visible brand logo. The back panel is perforated for ventilation and includes a slot for a power cord or antenna, as well as a label area. The sides and top are smooth, showcasing the wood texture, while the base is flat and stable for placement on surfaces. The overall design is classic and functional, evoking mid-20th-century aesthetics.
description: A vintage wooden tabletop radio receiver with a speaker grille, tuning knobs, and a frequency display on the front panel.
description_view_0: Front view showing the speaker grille, three control knobs, frequency display, and brand logo.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the smooth, polished wooden side panel with no visible controls or features.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing a perforated back panel for ventilation, a slot for a power cord or antenna, and a label area.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the other smooth wooden side panel, similar to view 1.
